channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: ‘Substantial insurance support’ pivotal as 20,000 seafarers trapped in worsening Strait of Hormuz crisis
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: PCRIC makes second drought parametric payout to Tonga
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Wright Flood to assume First Insurance Company of Hawaii’s NFIP book
item.description: Wright National Flood Insurance , part of Brown & Brown , announced an agreement to acquire the National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) policy book of First Insurance Company of Hawaii (FICOH). “First Insurance Company of Hawaii combines over a century of local market knowledge with the global strength of the Tokio Marine Group, making it a cornerstone of Hawaii’s insurance industry and a trusted partner for residents and businesses statewide,” said Patricia Templeton-Jones, CEO and chief program advocate of Wright National Flood Insurance Services. “We look forward to continuing their long history and providing their agents with our experience in the flood insurance industry.” “FICOH has built the largest NFIP book in Hawaii thanks to the dedication and expertise of our flood underwriting team over many years.

item.title: Kyber Selected as a PropertyCasualty360 2026 Insurance Luminaries Winner
item.description: Kyber has been named to PropertyCasualty360’s Insurance PropertyCasualty360’s Insurance Luminaries Class of 2026 , in the categories of Workplace Culture, Rising Star of the Year for Founder and CEO Arvind Sontha, and Excellence in Marketing/PR for Anna Conger. The 2026 winners were selected by a panel of industry experts based on how well they stated and achieved goals with regards to the nomination category; how impactful their work has been; how dedicated the nominee has been to furthering modernization and humanization in the P&C insurance business; and how committed and dedicated the nominee has been to high ethical standards, service and excellence. That’s why I am doubly excited to spotlight more individuals and organizations through the 2026 Insurance Luminaries Awards program and inaugural event.

item.title: Longbrook Hires Tanya Golikova for U.S. Tax Liability
item.description: Source: Longbrook.) Longbrook Insurance (London) has appointed Tanya Golikova Head of North America Tax Liability, strengthening its U.S. (Tanya Golikova, Head of North America Tax Liabiltiy, Longbrook Insurance. “Tanya brings a wealth of experience to our tax liability line of business which will be invaluable as our business continues to grow.” Longbrook says its transactional liability business is supported by long-term underwriting capacity, giving clients and brokers access to specialist underwriting expertise and a secure, highly rated balance sheet.

item.title: Aon Appoints Reville Casualty Catastrophe Leader
item.description: USC Marshall faculty site.) Aon (Dublin) has appointed Bob Reville Senior Managing Director, Casualty Catastrophe Market Leader for Aon’s Risk Capital, effective July 15, 2026. “We are building a casualty catastrophe market for latent risk that can match the property catastrophe market, expanding coverage and opening new opportunities across the insurance and capital markets,” comments Reville. Reville will support Aon insurer clients in addressing emerging and established casualty risks across product and environmental lines, according to an Aon statement.

item.title: Amwins Acquires Jackson-Lloyd
item.description: (Image source: Jackson-Lloyd homepage.) Amwins (Charlotte, N.C.) has acquired Jackson-Lloyd (Longview, Texas), a specialist MGA focused on occupational loss protection. Jackson-Lloyd will join Amwins Specialty Casualty Solutions, a full-service MGA and specialty insurance program creator, according to an Amwins statement. Amwins says its technology, data analytics, actuarial capabilities and national distribution network will support continued growth of Jackson-Lloyd’s product suite.

item.title: Insurers Avoid $664 Million Hit From Nord Stream Pipeline Blasts
item.description: The insurers of the Nord Stream pipelines don’t have to pay €580 million ($662 million) after a judge ruled that the sabotage of the pipes bringing Russian gas into Europe was the consequence of the war in Ukraine. Nord Stream AG, the Swiss company that owned the structure, sued its insurers in London following an explosion that ruptured three pipelines and dented another in September 2022. “I find that if any of the possible perpetrators carried out the sabotage, the war would have been a ‘significant’ cause of their actions.” Top photo: The release of gas emanating from a leak on the Nord Stream 2 gas pipeline in the Baltic Sea in a handout photo provided by Swedish Coast Guard in 2022.

item.title: East Coast Cities More Dangerous for Drivers According to New Allstate Report
item.description: New analysis highlights how location and daily driving habits impact road safety, according to Allstate’s 2026 America’s Best Drivers Report. Boston drivers are 189% more likely to experience a collision than the national average, the data showed. Drivers in the safest cities are nearly four times less likely to experience a crash.

item.title: Next stage of ILS growth requires international and peril diversification: Howden Re
item.description: The insurance-linked securities (ILS) market remains in a strong position, but expanding into international markets and less conventional perils will be critical for the market’s next stage of growth, according to executives at reinsurance broker Howden Re, who note that this expansion is vital both to unlock diversification benefits and to help close global protection gaps. Looking ahead, Atkinson cautioned that while the ILS market is in a strong position, the market’s next stage will depend heavily on disciplined capacity deployment, noting that the sector remains heavily concentrated around familiar risks, particularly US property-catastrophe. Over time, greater expansion into international markets and less conventional perils will be important, both for the diversification benefits this offers and for the role ILS play in helping to close protection gaps,” Atkinson explained.

item.title: California utility LADWP gets $100m 123 Lights Re wildfire cat bond pricing at low-end
item.description: The Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) has successfully priced its fourth catastrophe bond sponsorship today and so secured the targeted $100 million of California wildfire protection from the 123 Lights Re Ltd. Californian utility the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) came back to the catastrophe bond market in June, with an initial target to add another $100 million of multi-year wildfire insurance protection from the capital markets . Once settled, this will become the fourth catastrophe bond sponsored by the LADWP, with the utility having first entered the market in 2020, since returning in 2021 and 2025 for more cat bond-backed wildfire insurance.

item.title: Autonomous sees slower commercial pricing, mixed growth signals for US P&C insurers
item.description: Autonomous, an independent investment research firm, expects the second-quarter 2026 earnings season for US property and casualty (P&C) insurers to centre on slowing commercial insurance pricing, the balance between profitability and growth in personal lines, and the ability of insurance brokers to maintain organic growth in a softer market environment. However, Autonomous believes investor attention will increasingly shift towards the pace of pricing declines, with commercial insurance rates continuing to moderate across both admitted and excess and surplus markets. Although the effect of lower pricing has not yet been fully reflected in underwriting margins, Autonomous notes that analysts’ premium growth forecasts have become less optimistic since the start of the year.

item.title: Aon names Bob Reville Senior MD, Casualty Catastrophe Market Leader for Risk Capital
item.description: Aon, a global professional services company that provides risk, retirement and health solutions to organisations worldwide, has announced the appointment of Bob Reville as Senior Managing Director, Casualty Catastrophe Market Leader within Aon’s Risk Capital business, effective from 15 July. This is an exciting step forward in strengthening how we bring together casualty expertise, analytics and market innovation to solve increasingly complex client challenges.” Reville added: “We are building a casualty catastrophe market for latent risk that can match the property catastrophe market, expanding coverage and opening new opportunities across the insurance and capital markets. Reville will bring extensive experience in casualty modelling and predictive analytics to support the company’s insurer clients as they respond to an increasingly complex risk environment.

item.title: People Moves: Shepherd Insurance Names Hill as CIO
item.description: Shepherd Insurance announced the appointment of Collin Hill as the company’s first-ever chief information officer (CIO), a new leadership role created to support Shepherd’s continued growth and commitment to operational excellence. As CIO, Hill will be based at Shepherd’s headquarters in Carmel, Indiana, where he will lead Shepherd’s technology strategy, focusing on advancing technology capabilities, enhancing the use of data, and streamlining processes across the business. Hill brings more than two decades of technology and leadership experience spanning the private sector, higher education, and government, advancing into IT leadership roles before serving as a virtual CIO for small and mid-sized businesses.

item.title: Fatal Boat Capsizing Claims Three Lives in Wisconsin’s Geneva Lake
item.description: Three people died and seven others were rescued Friday after a boat capsized in Geneva Lake as a strong storm passed through southern Wisconsin, an official said. Geneva Lake, in southern Wisconsin, attracts tourists from the Chicago area and other parts. Police in the city of Lake Geneva are investigating, Hausner said.

item.title: Iowa Insurance Agent Charged With Identity Theft, Fraud
item.description: A Walcott, Iowa insurance agent has been charged with over 20 felonies for allegedly opening insurance policies under the names of family who had no knowledge that the polices were taken out, the Iowa Insurance Division announced. Blythe has been charged with 24 counties, including 11 counts of forgery, a Class D felony, and 10 counts of identity theft – over $1,500 and under $10,000, also a Class D felony. Stanly Blythe, 61, was arrested on June 26 and booked into the Scott County Jail, where he was being held on a $75,000 cash-only bond.

item.title: Majesco Acquires Vitech to Expand Across Insurance and Pension Technology Markets
item.description: The post Majesco Acquires Vitech to Expand Across Insurance and Pension Technology Markets appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Insurance software provider Majesco is acquiring pension and benefits administration specialist Vitech, in a deal backed by Thoma Bravo and CVC that broadens its reach across the Group & Benefits, Retirement & Pension, and Property & Casualty markets. Insurance software provider Majesco is acquiring pension and benefits administration specialist Vitech, in a deal backed by Thoma Bravo and CVC that broadens its reach across the Group & Benefits, Retirement & Pension, and Property & Casualty markets.

item.title: 'SaaSpocalypse' Debate Misses the Real Issue
item.description: There has been much wild speculation about the future of software-as-a-service (SaaS) applications in light of artificial intelligence (AI), with suggestions that a "SaaSpocalypse" will occur due to AI sweeping away the need for core SaaS-based systems. AI won't replace SaaS platforms, but vendors must prove their value by delivering measurable business outcomes for insurers. This is not very helpful for insurance companies weighing up their future IT adoption strategies.

item.title: Zurich and YAS launch embedded insurance for robots in Hong Kong
item.description: YAS announced a collaboration with Zurich to promote the use of AI and robotics in Hong Kong through an embedded insurance solution. With this partnership, we hope to bring together our respective strengths, using insurance to support technological development and to further enable digital transformation and smart operations for businesses in Hong Kong.” – Eric Hui, CEO of Zurich Insurance (Greater China). Under the partnership, Zurich will provide micro-insurance that is embedded into YAS’s robotics sales and service offerings.

item.title: AXA XL appoints Matt Rodliff as Chief Claims Officer, Americas
item.description: AXA XL has appointed Matt Rodliff as Chief Claims Officer, Americas, effective July 13. Based in Boston, he will report to Lucy Pilko, AXA XL’s CEO, Americas, and AXA XL’s Chief Administrative Officer and Global Chief Claims Officer Pam Urueta. DiVirgilio’s retirement follows an 18-year tenure at AXA XL, most recently serving as Chief Claims Officer, Americas.

item.title: World Absorbs Historic Iran War Oil Supply Loss, But Depleted Stocks Bring Risks
item.description: The world has absorbed with surprising ease the loss of over a billion barrels of oil supply since the Iran war began, but, with long-term peace elusive and buffer reserves now drained, it still faces the looming risk of future price spikes. At its worst, the headline supply loss was 14 million barrels per day. And countries around the world likely pulled around 1 billion barrels of oil from their reserves, including via an IEA-led record stocks release.

item.title: Engine Failure Not a Factor in Fiery Skydiving Plane Crash That Killed 12
item.description: Federal safety investigators said in a new preliminary report that they found no indication that engine failure caused the fiery crash of a plane on a skydiving outing last month in Missouri that killed all 12 people aboard, including several very experienced jumpers. In fact, NTSB said it appeared that the engine of the single-engine turboprop plane had been producing power at the time of the crash. The report said there were no indications of any precrash mechanical malfunctions or failures in the engine that would have prevented the normal operation of the plane.

item.title: UN Forecasters Say Strong El Niño Expected to Develop Rapidly Over Coming Months
item.description: The United Nations weather agency on Friday raised its forecast for the rapid emergence of a strong El Niño in the coming months, warning that the phenomenon is likely to drive global temperatures higher. El Niño is a periodic warming of sea surface temperatures in the central and eastern Pacific Ocean, which typically lasts between nine and 12 months, that can drive up global temperatures and increase the risk of extreme weather, according to the World Meteorological Organization (WMO). “El Niño conditions have emerged in the Equatorial Pacific, and there is a remarkable agreement between forecast models that this will be a strong El Niño ,” said Alvaro Silva, a scientist at the WMO.

item.title: Australia’s competition regulator probes Allianz-nib travel insurance tie-up
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: A Year Of Misplaced Fear (And Why It’s Time For Investors To Leave The Crowd)
item.description: Investors’ flight to perceived safety fundamentally misunderstands the risk profile of the moment. Crunchbase data shows that through April of this year, 80% of all U.S. But the apparent “wisdom” of the crowd persists — invest in the big name funds.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Cleantech Startup Funding Stabilizes As Energy Demand Grows
item.description: Cleantech isn’t the hottest space for startup funding these days. Nonetheless, amid a period of soaring electricity demand , rising EV adoption rates, and accelerating progress in fusion and other fields, cleantech investment activity isn’t slowing down. Even taking into account recent gains, however, cleantech funding remains far below its former peak in 2021 and 2022.

item.title: Cat bond market coupon yield rises slightly to 9.46%, but softening continues: Plenum
item.description: While the overall catastrophe bond market yield or coupon available to investors continued to rise through the month of June 2026, Plenum Investments noted that softening remains the dominant trend across new cat bond issuance. The trend for the overall cat bond market yield coupon has been to rise steadily higher through 2026. Seasonality continues to be muted so far, with a number of factors pulling against each other in the catastrophe bond market.

item.title: Catastrophe bond market records that were broken in H1 2026
item.description: The first-half of 2026 saw another record period of issuance in the catastrophe bond market, as the use of cat bonds to source reinsurance protection continues to increase and the investor base appetite for new cat bond investment opportunities grows. Having published our new report that analyses second-quarter and first-half 2026 catastrophe bond issuance last week, we wanted to follow-up and highlight some of the market’s impressive achievements from H1. For a detailed look at Q2 and H1 2026 catastrophe bond market activity download our latest report here .

item.title: AIG names Christine Williams as Head of Global Client and Broker Relationships
item.description: (AIG), a US-based insurance company operating internationally across general insurance and risk solutions, has announced the appointment of Christine Williams as Head of Global Client and Broker Relationships, effective 1 September 2026. Hancock said: “Christine Williams is a highly accomplished insurance executive recognised across the industry for her track record of driving growth and strengthening client relationships. In this newly created position at AIG, Williams will oversee the organisation’s global relationships with major clients and distribution partners, coordinating across AIG’s businesses to provide its capabilities, technical expertise and insurance solutions.

item.title: GSSP funding to strengthen SEADRIF climate and disaster risk finance across Southeast Asia
item.description: The Global Shield Solutions Platform (GSSP) is providing support to the Southeast Asia Disaster Risk Insurance Facility Insurance Company (SEADRIF) to expand Climate and Disaster Risk Finance and Insurance (CDRFI) capabilities across the region. With the funding of GSSP, a multi-donor fund that provides quick, pre-arranged insurance and emergency funding to climate-vulnerable developing countries, SEADRIF will implement a programme of insurer engagement, risk data analytics and knowledge-sharing activities. “This initiative will strengthen engagement with SEADRIF member countries and support the development of more locally relevant approaches to disaster risk financing.

item.title: Niyam adds Ritesh Shah as Director, Engineering and Construction
item.description: Niyam Group, an Indian-focused reinsurance platform, has appointed Ritesh Shah as Director of Engineering and Construction. He has come to shape the Engineering & Construction portfolio from the ground up, with conviction and with care. Shah brings the depth to write complex, large-scale risks, which is what is currently required for India’s infrastructure ambition, explained the firm.

item.title: Japan-Linked Ships Escaping Hormuz in Convoy Through Iran Route
item.description: Import-dependent Japan has repeatedly called on Iran to ensure the free transit through Hormuz. are heading out of the Strait of Hormuz using a route close to Iran, including some of the last trapped oil tankers to exit the waterway. Iran has sought to assert control over Hormuz, through which a fifth of all oil moved before the war, since it reached an interim peace deal with the US last month.

item.title: Europe Faces Weeks of Extreme Heat as Fire and Power Risks Build
item.description: Western Europe is bracing for the summer’s third major heat wave, fueling dangerous wildfire conditions and straining power systems across the region. Climate change is intensifying periods of extreme heat on the fastest-warming continent. A high-pressure weather system is set to bring heat wave conditions this week to France, southern England and the Iberian Peninsula, according to weather models and government forecasters.

item.title: Viewpoint: Methuselah Mutuals
item.description: This piece identifies secrets behind the success of “Methuselah” mutuals and points to lessons the rest of the insurance industry can learn from them. Those denied insurance coverage responded by forming their own companies, organizing them as cooperatives, or “mutuals.” Unlike shareholder-owned stock companies, mutual companies protected policyholders by giving them ownership interest in the business. Whereas stock companies, especially those that are publicly traded, must report their earnings every quarter and do their utmost to generate rising book value per share, mutuals are managed with the future in mind.

item.title: Sun Life Completes $350 Million Acquisition of Bell Partners
item.description: Sun Life Financial has completed its acquisition of US multifamily real estate manager Bell Partners for $350 million, folding a long-established property platform into its BGO real estate arm as the insurer deepens its push into asset management. Sun Life Financial has completed its acquisition of US multifamily real estate manager Bell Partners for $350 million, folding a long-established property platform into its BGO real estate arm as the insurer deepens its push into asset management. Approximately 80% of the consideration was paid in Sun Life common shares rather than cash, a structure that limits immediate capital outflow, retains liquidity for further acquisitions, and gives Bell Partners’ principals a continuing equity stake in the acquiring platform.

item.title: Lloyd’s Consortium Model Gains Momentum as RSUM Deal Signals Market Shift
item.description: As delegated authority moves towards becoming the majority of Lloyd’s business, the RSUM deal illustrates how consortium-led facilities, supported by digital trading infrastructure, are fast becoming a central mechanism for deploying capacity in the specialty market, rather than a peripheral option. Lloyd’s has named consortium-led facilities a major growth area for 2026, and the market is responding. Ryan Specialty Underwriting Managers (RSUM) completed a series of Lloyd’s consortium stamps on 6 July, with six syndicates taking a combined 15% share across RSUM’s global P&C delegated underwriting portfolio.

item.title: More than 20,000 Travelers employees now regularly use AI
item.description: More than 20,000 employees regularly use AI tools, dozens of generative AI applications are in production, millions of transactions are automated, and agentic AI is embedded across business operations. Travelers and the Travelers Foundation contributed approximately $24 million to local communities in 2025, while employees logged more than 125,000 volunteer hours. As of May 2026, more than 150 Travelers employees held civic leadership roles, including firefighters, first responders, election officials, and zoning commissioners.

item.title: John Lewis Money relaunches its car insurance offering
item.description: John Lewis Money, the financial services arm of UK retailer John Lewis Partnership, has relaunched its car insurance offering. John Lewis Money announced it had become a licensed insurance broker in March this year and it relaunched its home insurance offering shortly after. We’re excited to launch this model across our car offer, and have partnered with trusted names in the sector to deliver for customers.” – Gary Davess, Director of Insurance for John Lewis Money.

item.title: MGA Bamboo launches California homeowners program
item.description: Bamboo Insurance has launched Essential, a California HO-2 homeowners program designed to offer lower-cost coverage focused on core protection. The launch follows Bamboo’s recent expansion of its California homeowners portfolio, including the introduction of a condominium unit-owners (HO-6) policy and the launch of its E&S Signature program in Texas through an MGA-sponsored sidecar with Accredited. The program is available for owner-occupied primary residences as well as seasonal and secondary homes.

item.title: Banco Falabella Perú: los ataques silenciosos desafían a la banca
item.description: Un intruso puede permanecer durante meses dentro de la infraestructura de una entidad financiera sin ser detectado, una tendencia donde los atacantes están apostando por accesos silenciosos para explorar sistemas y escalar privilegios antes de ejecutar un ataque, advierten desde Banco Falabella Perú. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: AI’s Impact: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly
item.description: The weakness stands out against an otherwise robust labor market that created more than 113,000 jobs monthly this year through May — a number that would have been much higher if the banking and tech industries hadn’t dragged down the overall total. Related: OpenAI’s Altman Says AI Unlikely to Lead to ‘Jobs Apocalypse’ After investing heavily in artificial intelligence, tech companies are now increasingly citing AI as a factor for cutting their workforce. A decline in payrolls in the financial-activities and information sectors — where AI adoption rates have been fastest — has accelerated in 2026, to 28,000 per month on average based on government data.

item.title: Pickups Dominate Top 20 Vehicles Sold in US in The Second Quarter
item.description: Pickup trucks remained among the top vehicles sold in the U.S., but a few sedans including the Honda Accord were popular purchases in the second quarter, a new list shows. Reuters compiled a list the 20 top-selling vehicles in the United States in Q2 2026, as reported by automakers and ranked by total units. Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox.

item.title: Willis appoints Gary McMullen as senior director for workplace risk
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aviso Specialty hires Kerin Rathgeber as trade credit head
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Niyam Group taps Ritesh Shah to drive India engineering, construction buildout
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA publishes landmark review into impact of AI on retail financial services
item.description: The review sets out how AI could reshape retail financial services for consumers, firms, markets and regulators by 2030 and beyond. Drawing on views from across the financial services landscape, the report identifies 4 major AI‑driven shifts likely to impact retail financial services: the transformation of firm operations; the evolution of consumer journeys; the reshaping of competition and market power; and the amplification of fraud and cyber risks. The Review concludes that AI is likely to become a defining force in retail financial services, transforming how firms operate, how consumers make financial decisions and how markets function.

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending July 5th 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending July 5th 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending July 5th 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Cat bond fund UCITS average 0.62% return in June. 12-month rolling-return 10.22%
item.description: According to the latest data from the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices , catastrophe bond fund strategies in the UCITS format averaged a 0.62% return for the month of June 2026 which beat last year, while the rolling-twelve month return rose slightly to 10.22% as a result of the stronger month. As a result, the average year-to-date performance of the UCITS format catastrophe bond fund strategies rose from 2.18% at May 29th to now 2.91% as of June 26th 2026, the latest data available and closest we have to a half-year return for these catastrophe bond funds. You can analyse the Plenum CAT Bond UCITS Fund Indices in our charts: Interestingly, for June the performance advantage switched to the lower-risk cohort of cat bond funds in the UCITS format, with these averaging a 0.66% return for the month, while the higher-risk cat bond funds averaged 0.60%.

item.title: AXA XL’s new catastrophe bond is the first ever to cover US terrorism risk
item.description: As a result, this Galileo Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond is the first ever to provide a sponsor with protection against terrorism events in the United States. We’ve now learned this deal was particularly innovative and set some new market firsts, including becoming the first US terrorism cat bond ever. Most prominent are the four Baltic PCC Limited catastrophe bonds issued for the UK terrorism reinsurer Pool Re , as well as the Athéna I Reinsurance DAC cat bond for the French terrorism risk pool Gareat.

item.title: Klapton Re welcomes Mukuma Musenge as Chief Technology Officer
item.description: Zambia-based reinsurer Klapton Reinsurance has announced the appointment of Mukuma Musenge as Chief Technology Officer. His career also includes a three-year tenure at Kuala Tech as Chief Executive Officer, as well as close to two years as Information Technology Manager at Klapton Re. In his new position, Musenge will lead Klapton Re’s technology function, focusing on driving the development of integrated systems and data-led solutions that improve efficiency, enhance customer experience, and support smarter business decision-making.
